title: Contract termination: billing cessation & cost allocation playbook
preview:
Summary:
This playbook defines standardized procedures for stopping invoicing, allocating final costs, issuing credits or refunds, and updating forecasts and revenue recognition after contract termination. It covers hybrid contracts (hosted + dedicated + private), pre-paid commitments, and purchase-order disputes th...
text:
Summary:
This playbook defines standardized procedures for stopping invoicing, allocating final costs, issuing credits or refunds, and updating forecasts and revenue recognition after contract termination. It covers hybrid contracts (hosted + dedicated + private), pre-paid commitments, and purchase-order disputes that impact billing cutoffs. The goal is to minimize revenue leakage, preserve audit evidence, and give clear operational ownership for the termination window.

Scope:
- Applies to all customer contracts (hosted API, Redwood Dedicated, Redwood Private) that enter contract termination, expiration, or early-termination workflows.
- Covers billing cessation, final invoice generation, post-termination credits/refunds, accounting journal guidance, forecast adjustments, and procurement handoffs.
- Does NOT replace legal termination clauses; legal must be consulted for contract-spec...

title: Capacity cost hedging and billing coordination guide

The goal is to reduce P&L volatility from usage seasonality and to ensure procurement...
text:
Overview:

This guide describes Redwood Inference's standardized approach for aligning capacity purchasing (GPU/pool reservations and cloud commit tiers), short‑term hedging strategies, and billing/revenue-recognition coordination. The goal is to reduce P&L volatility from usage seasonality and to ensure procurement, legal, finance, and product teams share a synchronized playbook for commits, overage handling, and invoice timing.

Scope:
- Applies to Hosted API and Dedicated capacity products; Private deployments follow a separate procurement cadence but should reference the hedging principles below.
- Covers forecasting windows of 1 month, 3 months, and 12 months.
- Includes guidance for monthly usage commits, reserved GPU capacity, spot/burst usage policies, and contract features that materially affect revrec (e.g., non-refundable prepayments, renewal triggers).

title: Mitigation: Provisional credit reversal causing autopay hold (RevRec & Billing)
preview:
## Purpose
This page documents the operational mitigation and finance/legal guidance for incidents where a support-issued provisional credit is automatically reversed during reconciliation, leading to a negative invoice balance and an autopay hold. It consolidates learnings from SUP-121212 (Acme Health) and establis...
text:
## Purpose
This page documents the operational mitigation and finance/legal guidance for incidents where a support-issued provisional credit is automatically reversed during reconciliation, leading to a negative invoice balance and an autopay hold. It consolidates learnings from SUP-121212 (Acme Health) and establishes a repeatable response for RevRec, Billing Engineering, and Customer Support.

## Background (what happened)
In SUP-121212, a provisional credit issued for hourly smoothing was reversed automatically during an overnight/hourly reconciliation run. The reversal produced a negative balance on the customer invoice and triggered an automated autopay hold. For a dedicated customer, the payment-hold state also restricted production API keys and degraded integrations.

### Reference timeline (example case: Acme Health)
- 2026-03-10 22:14 UTC: Support issued provisional credit (R...

title: Invoice "Overage Adjustment" After Quota Threshold Change — Proration & Rate-Lookup RCA (2026)
preview:
## Summary
A dedicated-plan customer (Maple Health) reported an unexpected invoice increase immediately after changing their quota threshold from **1M tokens to 2M tokens** via the Console UI on **2026-03-09**. The subsequent invoice generated on **2026-03-10** included a large line item labeled **"overage adjustmen...
text:
## Summary
A dedicated-plan customer (Maple Health) reported an unexpected invoice increase immediately after changing their quota threshold from **1M tokens to 2M tokens** via the Console UI on **2026-03-09**. The subsequent invoice generated on **2026-03-10** included a large line item labeled **"overage adjustment"**.

Engineering confirmed the charge was caused by a **rate-lookup fallback to a legacy/default mapping** during a narrow failure window in the quota metadata write path. The invoicing pipeline proceeded using the fallback rate rather than retrying rate lookup after metadata became available.

Finance issued a manual credit and Engineering shipped a hotfix to prevent recurrence.

---

## Customer & Scope
- Customer: **Maple Health**
- Tier: **Dedicated**
- Environment: **prod (us-east)**
- Affected model(s) observed in ledger context: **gpt-4o-mini**
- Primary artifact: ...

title: Customer Commitment Variance Resolution and Forecasting SOP
preview:
Overview
This Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) outlines the end-to-end process for identifying, triaging, and resolving variance between contracted customer commitments and our revenue/usage forecasts. It also documents how procurement changes, contract amendments, and billing timing anomalies should flow into the...
text:
Overview
This Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) outlines the end-to-end process for identifying, triaging, and resolving variance between contracted customer commitments and our revenue/usage forecasts. It also documents how procurement changes, contract amendments, and billing timing anomalies should flow into the financial forecast and revenue recognition pipeline. The goal is to reduce forecast drift to < 2% quarter-on-quarter for enterprise contracted ARR and to ensure consistent handoffs between Sales, Legal, Customer Success, and Finance.

Scope
- Applies to all customer commitments that trigger revenue recognition or forecast adjustments: new enterprise commitments, dedicated capacity reservations, committed usage plans, multi-year subscriptions, and large one-off professional services agreements.
- Excludes ad-hoc usage consumption with no contractual minimums (these are hand...

text:
Purpose:

Provide concrete governance for how Redwood ties multi-month capacity contracts to budgeting, procurement, legal review, and revenue recognition. This guidance is intended for Finance, Legal, Procurement, Sales, and Platform teams responsible for capacity commitments.

Executive summary:
- Treat all multi-month reserved GPU capacity agreements as cross-functional commitments that require synchronized forecasting, procurement timing, and revrec mapping.
- Establish predictable thresholds and a clear approval chain to protect financial runway while enabling sales to secure competitive terms for customers.
- Define operational steps to recover value from unused capacity and to limit exposure from long-term discounts.

Definitions and thresholds:

text:
## 1. Purpose
This page defines the **formal billing meters** for Redwood Inference’s **Hosted API** (including OpenAI-compatible endpoints) and the rules used to compute:
- **Request counts**
- **Prompt (input) tokens**
- **Completion (output) tokens**
- **Billed tokens** (the chargeable unit for usage-based pricing)

It also specifies:
- Streaming semantics and disconnect behavior
- Retries, idempotency, and deduplication requirements (to prevent double-counting)
- Tool/function calling token attribution rules
- Rounding rules
- How meters map to the **billing ledger**, **customer Usage API**, and **invoices**

This spec is used by Engineering (telemetry + billing hooks), Finance/RevRec (reconciliation + close), RevOps/Product (pricing experiments), and Legal (usage definitions in contracts).

---

## 2. Scope

title: Dedicated Capacity Usage Meter Definition (GPU-seconds)
preview:
## Purpose
This document defines how Redwood Inference meters **Dedicated Capacity** usage for billing, revenue recognition support, and customer-facing usage reporting. The unit for Dedicated usage is **GPU-seconds**.

This definition is intentionally precise because small ambiguities (e.g., how idle time is counte...
text:
## Purpose
This document defines how Redwood Inference meters **Dedicated Capacity** usage for billing, revenue recognition support, and customer-facing usage reporting. The unit for Dedicated usage is **GPU-seconds**.

This definition is intentionally precise because small ambiguities (e.g., how idle time is counted, autoscaling transitions, or node flaps) can create invoice disputes and RevRec risk.

**Audience:** Finance/RevRec, Legal, RevOps, Product, and Engineering (Telemetry + Control Plane).

---

## Scope
### In scope
- Dedicated GPU pools provisioned to a single customer (data isolated).
- Metered usage for:
 - Overage beyond reserved commits (if applicable)
 - Burst capacity (if enabled)
 - Usage visibility and forecasting (even when fully covered by commit)
- Per-pool and per-day aggregation for invoices and usage exports.

### Out of scope
- Hosted API token/request meter...

text:
## Purpose
This playbook defines how Redwood Inference reconciles customer invoices back to underlying metering data.

It is designed to:
- Detect billing-impacting metering issues early (daily/weekly) and before invoices are sent (month-end).
- Provide a repeatable, audit-ready trail from: **request logs → usage events → billing ledger → invoice lines → customer usage exports**.
- Standardize discrepancy classification and remediation (forward fixes, credits, backfills) with clear sign-offs.

**Applies to:**
- Hosted API (tokens/requests, embeddings, reranking)
- Dedicated (GPU-seconds)

**Not covered:** tax, FX, payment collection, revenue allocation. For month-end close controls, see *Month-End Close: Usage Reconciliation Checklist*.

---

## Definitions (canonical)
**Billing Ledger:** The internal finance-grade table used to generate invoice line items. This is the **source of tru...

text:
## Purpose
This page defines how **Standard Cost Model (SCM)** outputs map to **FP&A unit economics** reporting for Redwood Inference.

It is written for Finance/FP&A, with implementation notes for Platform/Billing/Telemetry teams.

This doc answers:
- What the SCM is (and is not), from a finance perspective.
- Which SCM fields are **finance-reportable** vs **product/observability-only**.
- How to translate SCM outputs into FP&A metrics (COGS, gross margin, contribution margin, per-token unit costs).
- The **overhead allocation policy** for shared infrastructure and how it appears in SCM outputs.
- Reporting windows, cutoffs, backfills, and reconciliation rules.

## Scope and non-goals
### In scope
- Hosted API, Dedicated capacity, and Private (VPC/on-prem) deployments where Redwood has cost responsibility.
- GPU compute, CPU/other compute for serving, networking/egress, storage, and ...

preview:

The goal is to reduce off-budget spend, improve procurement predictability, and ensu...
text:
Summary
------
This document defines the Forecast-Aligned Purchasing Authorization Framework (FAPAF): a single, auditable process that ties purchase approvals to active budget forecasts and anticipated revenue recognition timelines. The goal is to reduce off-budget spend, improve procurement predictability, and ensure finance has timely inputs for RevRec and billing handoffs.

Scope
-----
- Applies to all Redwood Inference employees and contractors initiating purchase requests (POs, contracts, reserved capacity, and subscriptions).
- Covers capital (CapEx) and operating (OpEx) purchases that affect monthly/annual forecasts or revenue recognition timelines.
- Integrates Budget Owners, Procurement, Legal, and Revenue Operations (RevOps).

Key principles
--------------
1. Forecast-first approvals: every purchase > $10k USD or that creates a multi-period obligation must reference a foreca...

title: Procurement Approval Matrix and Billing-Trigger Guide

It is intended for Finance, Legal, Procurement owners, and Engineering or Product teams who ...
text:
Purpose
-------
This page defines Redwood Inference's standardized procurement approval matrix, the lifecycle steps for contract execution, and the precise billing triggers that feed the revenue recognition (revrec) process. It is intended for Finance, Legal, Procurement owners, and Engineering or Product teams who initiate supplier commitments. The goal is to reduce cycle time, ensure correct accounting treatment at go-live, and avoid revenue/billing misalignment across hosted, dedicated, and private product lines.

Scope
-----
Applies to: all third-party software and hardware purchases, cloud/hosted commitments, professional services, and vendor contracts that create a legal obligation or financial commitment for Redwood Inference (including pilot agreements and proof-of-concept statements of work).
Excludes: petty cash, employee expense reimbursements, and subscriptions procured on...

title: Phased Invoicing and Commitment Optimization: Guidance for Contracts, Billing, and RevRec
preview:

It is intended for Finan...
text:
Summary
-------
This document defines Redwood Inference's recommended approach for phased invoicing, proration, and revenue recognition for multi-period commitments (annual/multi-year) with variable usage and mixed billing components (committed capacity, overage, one-time fees, and credits). It is intended for Finance (RevOps), Legal, Sales, Procurement, and Billing engineering teams. The guidance balances predictable customer billing cadence with sound revrec accounting and clear operational procedures for reconciling committed usage and invoiced amounts.

Scope
-----
Applies to:
- New and renewed commercial contracts with any committed capacity or prepaid credits.
- Multi-year subscriptions, reserved/dedicated capacity contracts, and hybrid pricing (commit + usage).
Excludes: purely pay-as-you-go short-term trial invoices (covered in billing SOPs).

-----------
- Commitm...

title: Blended Cost Methodology v1 (Unit Economics Dashboard)

text:
## Purpose
This document defines the Finance-aligned methodology for computing **Blended Cost per 1M Output Tokens** (“blended $/1M output tokens”) as used in Redwood’s internal **Unit Economics Dashboard**. It is intended to:
- Provide a consistent metric definition that reconciles (within agreed tolerances) to Finance reporting and infrastructure spend inputs.
- Establish the source of truth for the GPU cost rate card and its update/versioning cadence.
- Specify allocation rules (including shared overhead and idle capacity treatment) so cross-team analysis uses the same assumptions.
- Define required reconciliation checks and sign-off gates for executive reporting.

This is **v1** of the methodology. Changes must follow the versioning process described below.

## Metric definition (v1)
### Primary KPI
**Blended Cost per 1M Output Tokens**

**Formula:**
- **Blended $/1M output tokens...
